# servicenow_widget (Data Source)

`servicenow_widget` data source can be used to retrieve information of a single Widget in ServiceNow by name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up an existing Widget in ServiceNow by name.
data "servicenow_widget" "example" {
  name = "MyExistingWidget"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Display name of the widget.

### Read-Only

- `client_script` (String) Client-side AngularJS controller script for the widget. ServiceNow provides a default value if not specified.
- `controller_as` (String) The AngularJS controller alias used in the widget template.
- `css` (String) CSS styles applied to the widget.
- `data_table` (String) The ServiceNow table the widget fetches data from. ServiceNow defaults to 'sp_instance' if not specified.
- `demo_data` (String) Demo data in JSON format used when previewing the widget.
- `description` (String) Description of the widget.
- `has_preview` (Boolean) If set to 'true', the widget supports preview mode.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `identifier` (String) Unique identifier for the widget used in scripts and portal pages.
- `link_function` (String) Link function script executed when the widget is linked. ServiceNow provides a default value if not specified.
- `option_schema` (String) JSON schema defining the configurable options for the widget.
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `public` (Boolean) If set to 'true', this widget is accessible without authentication.
- `roles` (String) Comma-separated list of roles required to use the widget.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `server_script` (String) Server-side script executed when the widget loads. ServiceNow provides a default value if not specified.
- `template` (String) The HTML template body of the widget.
